I did verify the pinned TB-2 submodule state by running `git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2`, which checked out commit `53ff2b87d621bdb97b455671f2bd9728b7d86c11`, and I confirmed `scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-full.json` points at that dataset commit and `scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-qwen3-6-27b.yaml` targets the local Vidar endpoint. What remains for a follow-up is a dedicated benchmark window with exclusive Vidar/omlx access so the full 267-cell command from the bead can be run, aggregated, counted for `graded_pass`, and committed only if the matrix reaches at least 27 passes.
